upright_meaning_verbatim: Skill, diplomacy, address, subtlety; sickness, pain, loss, disaster, snares of enemies; self-confidence, will; the Querent, if male.
reversed_meaning_verbatim: Physician, Magus, mental disease, disgrace, disquiet.
source_title: A. E. Waite, The Pictorial Key to the Tarot, Part III §3
license: Public domain in US (Sacred Texts/Wikisource public-domain notice)

upright_meaning_verbatim: Secrets, mystery, the future as yet unrevealed; the woman who interests the Querent, if male; the Querent herself, if female; silence, tenacity; mystery, wisdom, science.
reversed_meaning_verbatim: Passion, moral or physical ardour, conceit, surface knowledge.
source_title: A. E. Waite, The Pictorial Key to the Tarot, Part III §3
license: Public domain in US (Sacred Texts/Wikisource public-domain notice)

upright_meaning_verbatim: Fruitfulness, action, initiative, length of days; the unknown, clandestine; also difficulty, doubt, ignorance.
reversed_meaning_verbatim: Light, truth, the unravelling of involved matters, public rejoicings; according to another reading, vacillation.
source_title: A. E. Waite, The Pictorial Key to the Tarot, Part III §3
license: Public domain in US (Sacred Texts/Wikisource public-domain notice)

upright_meaning_verbatim: Stability, power, protection, realization; a great person; aid, reason, conviction; also authority and will.
reversed_meaning_verbatim: Benevolence, compassion, credit; also confusion to enemies, obstruction, immaturity.
source_title: A. E. Waite, The Pictorial Key to the Tarot, Part III §3
license: Public domain in US (Sacred Texts/Wikisource public-domain notice)

upright_meaning_verbatim: Marriage, alliance, captivity, servitude; by another account, mercy and goodness; inspiration; the man to whom the Querent has recourse.
reversed_meaning_verbatim: Society, good understanding, concord, overkindness, weakness.
source_title: A. E. Waite, The Pictorial Key to the Tarot, Part III §3
license: Public domain in US (Sacred Texts/Wikisource public-domain notice)

upright_meaning_verbatim: Attraction, love, beauty, trials overcome.
reversed_meaning_verbatim: Failure, foolish designs. Another account speaks of marriage frustrated and contrarieties of all kinds.
source_title: A. E. Waite, The Pictorial Key to the Tarot, Part III §3
license: Public domain in US (Sacred Texts/Wikisource public-domain notice)

upright_meaning_verbatim: Succour, providence also war, triumph, presumption, vengeance, trouble.
reversed_meaning_verbatim: Riot, quarrel, dispute, litigation, defeat.
source_title: A. E. Waite, The Pictorial Key to the Tarot, Part III §3
license: Public domain in US (Sacred Texts/Wikisource public-domain notice)

upright_meaning_verbatim: Power, energy, action, courage, magnanimity; also complete success and honours.
reversed_meaning_verbatim: Despotism, abuse if power, weakness, discord, sometimes even disgrace.
source_title: A. E. Waite, The Pictorial Key to the Tarot, Part III §3
license: Public domain in US (Sacred Texts/Wikisource public-domain notice)

upright_meaning_verbatim: Prudence, circumspection; also and especially treason, dissimulation, roguery, corruption.
reversed_meaning_verbatim: Concealment, disguise, policy, fear, unreasoned caution.
source_title: A. E. Waite, The Pictorial Key to the Tarot, Part III §3
license: Public domain in US (Sacred Texts/Wikisource public-domain notice)

upright_meaning_verbatim: Destiny, fortune, success, elevation, luck, felicity.
reversed_meaning_verbatim: Increase, abundance, superfluity.
source_title: A. E. Waite, The Pictorial Key to the Tarot, Part III §3
license: Public domain in US (Sacred Texts/Wikisource public-domain notice)

upright_meaning_verbatim: Equity, rightness, probity, executive; triumph of the deserving side in law.
reversed_meaning_verbatim: Law in all its departments, legal complications, bigotry, bias, excessive severity.
source_title: A. E. Waite, The Pictorial Key to the Tarot, Part III §3
license: Public domain in US (Sacred Texts/Wikisource public-domain notice)

upright_meaning_verbatim: Wisdom, circumspection, discernment, trials, sacrifice, intuition, divination, prophecy.
reversed_meaning_verbatim: Selfishness, the crowd, body politic.
source_title: A. E. Waite, The Pictorial Key to the Tarot, Part III §3
license: Public domain in US (Sacred Texts/Wikisource public-domain notice)

upright_meaning_verbatim: End, mortality, destruction, corruption also, for a man, the loss of a benefactor for a woman, many contrarieties; for a maid, failure of marriage projects.
reversed_meaning_verbatim: Inertia, sleep, lethargy, petrifaction, somnambulism; hope destroyed.
source_title: A. E. Waite, The Pictorial Key to the Tarot, Part III §3
license: Public domain in US (Sacred Texts/Wikisource public-domain notice)

upright_meaning_verbatim: Economy, moderation, frugality, management, accommodation.
reversed_meaning_verbatim: Things connected with churches, religions, sects, the priesthood, sometimes even the priest who will marry the Querent; also disunion, unfortunate combinations, competing interests.
source_title: A. E. Waite, The Pictorial Key to the Tarot, Part III §3
license: Public domain in US (Sacred Texts/Wikisource public-domain notice)

upright_meaning_verbatim: Ravage, violence, vehemence, extraordinary efforts, force, fatality; that which is predestined but is not for this reason evil.
reversed_meaning_verbatim: Evil fatality, weakness, pettiness, blindness.
source_title: A. E. Waite, The Pictorial Key to the Tarot, Part III §3
license: Public domain in US (Sacred Texts/Wikisource public-domain notice)

upright_meaning_verbatim: Misery, distress, indigence, adversity, calamity, disgrace, deception, ruin. It is a card in particular of unforeseen catastrophe.
reversed_meaning_verbatim: According to one account, the same in a lesser degree also oppression, imprisonment, tyranny.
source_title: A. E. Waite, The Pictorial Key to the Tarot, Part III §3
license: Public domain in US (Sacred Texts/Wikisource public-domain notice)

upright_meaning_verbatim: Loss, theft, privation, abandonment; another reading says-hope and bright prospects.
reversed_meaning_verbatim: Arrogance, haughtiness, impotence.
source_title: A. E. Waite, The Pictorial Key to the Tarot, Part III §3
license: Public domain in US (Sacred Texts/Wikisource public-domain notice)

upright_meaning_verbatim: Hidden enemies, danger, calumny, darkness, terror, deception, occult forces, error.
reversed_meaning_verbatim: Instability, inconstancy, silence, lesser degrees of deception and error.
source_title: A. E. Waite, The Pictorial Key to the Tarot, Part III §3
license: Public domain in US (Sacred Texts/Wikisource public-domain notice)

upright_meaning_verbatim: Material happiness, fortunate marriage, contentment.
reversed_meaning_verbatim: The same in a lesser sense.
source_title: A. E. Waite, The Pictorial Key to the Tarot, Part III §3
license: Public domain in US (Sacred Texts/Wikisource public-domain notice)

upright_meaning_verbatim: Change of position, renewal, outcome. Another account specifies total loss though lawsuit.
reversed_meaning_verbatim: Weakness, pusillanimity, simplicity; also deliberation, decision, sentence.
source_title: A. E. Waite, The Pictorial Key to the Tarot, Part III §3
license: Public domain in US (Sacred Texts/Wikisource public-domain notice)

upright_meaning_verbatim: Folly, mania, extravagance, intoxication, delirium, frenzy, bewrayment.
reversed_meaning_verbatim: Negligence, absence, distribution, carelessness, apathy, nullity, vanity.
source_title: A. E. Waite, The Pictorial Key to the Tarot, Part III §3
license: Public domain in US (Sacred Texts/Wikisource public-domain notice)

upright_meaning_verbatim: Assured success, recompense, voyage, route, emigration, flight, change of place.
reversed_meaning_verbatim: Inertia, fixity, stagnation, permanence.
source_title: A. E. Waite, The Pictorial Key to the Tarot, Part III §3
license: Public domain in US (Sacred Texts/Wikisource public-domain notice)
